steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to provide an example of a specific particle physicist whose research focuses on neutrinos. A correct answer must name a physicist and connect that physicist directly to neutrino research.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The research done on high-energy astrophysical neutrinos exemplifies the work particle physicists do to advance our understanding of subatomic particles.: Discusses neutrino research in general terms but never names a physicist. Fails the "example of a particle physicist" requirement. ✗
  • Choice B — By studying high-energy astrophysical neutrinos—to name just one example—particle physicists can learn more about neutrinos.: Again no named physicist. Describes what particle physicists do collectively, not an individual example. ✗
  • Choice C — Particle physicist Victor Halvorsen is known for his discovery of high-energy astrophysical neutrinos.: Names Victor Halvorsen, identifies him as a particle physicist, and links him specifically to neutrino research. Satisfies both parts of the goal. ✓
  • Choice D — Neutrinos are a type of subatomic particle that particle physicists are still trying to understand.: A general statement about neutrinos with no physicist named. Irrelevant to the goal. ✗
That matches Choice C — Particle physicist Victor Halvorsen is known for his discovery of high-energy astrophysical neutrinos..
